content_en:
I stood upright and was trembling. Then he commanded me:

“Return immediately to Ahwaz and as soon as you reach there, distribute these pearls among the Mujahideen.”

I respectfully said, ‘Caliph | will obey your orders.’ He looked at me, full of wrath and said:

“By Allah, if this necklace is not distributed among the Mujahideen, 1 will break your bones as well as those of the Commander-in-Chief.”

I raced my horse at breakneck speed and passing through those difficult roads, at last reached the presence of the Commander-inChief Salamah bin Qais -. I told him what had happened and said:

“O Salamabh if you want to save your life and mine, then please immediately distribute this necklace among the Mujahideen.”

He asked me what had happened and why I was so terrified. Then I gave him all the details of what had happened with me.

Salamah bin Qais # distributed the necklace in the Majlis (gathering) of the Mujahideen right then and there.

‘Utbah bin Ghazwan 2

“T ask for Allah protection from the condition

where I may think myself to be very great now, but in the sight of Allah I shall be

‘Utbah bin Ghazwan 2

Tall and well-built, with a radiant face an attractive personality, and a sturdy build - this was his physical appearance. He had polished manners and was a man who practised abstinence, lived a clean and pious life. In the field of battle he was a brave soldier who could inflict fatal wounds for the cause of Islam.

In 14 Hijrah, in obedience to the command of ‘Umar Farooq #%, he built a beautiful new city, Basra, on the banks of the river Dajlah. He was leader who guided his people wisely and well. He displayed his mastery with the sword as a Mujahid in the Battle of the Trenches, and the Battles of Badr, Uhud, Khaibar and Ta’if. He was also one of those who took part in the signing of the Treaty of Hudaibiah and was one of the fortunate and honoured ones who took part in the Bai’at Rizwan.

He was a great of the Muslim Ummah who entered Makkah triumphantly but modestly with the Prophet 3 and ten thousand other loyal Mujahideen who had risked their lives. In the Battle of Tabook he was a hardworking soldier who was one of the ‘Jaish Al- ‘Asrah’. He left lasting impressions of his courage and daring asa General. ‘Umar Farooq 4% selected him to lead the army to conquer the southern area of Iraq after a great deal of thought. He ordered him to command a smail contingent of the Muslim army to Iraq and Iran. These were his word of advice on this occasion:

“O 'Utbah, reach those limits of Arabia which are bordered by the lands of the non-Arabs, leading your men with complete trust in Allah 38. Always remember to keep before you the fear of Allah 38. You will have to deal with a cunning, deceitful and

hypocritical enemy. You will have to keep an alert mind; be courageous and wise in opposing him. Go and may Allah be your Support and Help.”

‘Utbah bin Ghazwan was a brilliant and popular young man of the tribe of Banu Mazan. He was the pride and envy of his people. During the pre-Islamic days his family was the ally of the tribe of Banu Nofil bin ‘Abd Manaf. When he was thirty years old, the news was doing the rounds of Makkah that the beloved son of Amenah, the grandson of ‘Abdul-Muttlib and the son of ‘Abdullah had declared that he was a Prophet. His Message is that there is only One True Allah. Only He is the Creator and the Master, and only He is the Provider and Helper and only He has control over Life and Death. When His claims became known there was a terrible tumult in Makkah.

The chieftains of the Quraish began to perpetrate the cruelest atrocities on the newly converted Muslims. Seeing the helplessness of his followers, the Prophet 3 ordered them to migrate towards Ethiopia. “Utbah bin Ghazwan 4 was a member of the second caravan of the oppressed people which left Makkah. After living there in peace and security for some time he returned, as he was missing the Prophet 38 whom he loved deeply and also he was feeling restless and homesick.

When the Prophet 3% decided to migrate to Al-Madinah because the cruelty of the Quraish had become intolerable, ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan again started to miss him. He was waiting for an opportunity to enjoy the company and friendship of his beloved Prophet # as early as possible.

In Shawwal, 1* Hijrah some youths set out armed under the leadership of ‘Ikrimah bin Abu Jahl to put pressure on the newly settled Muslims. After travelling for some time they reached a place called Rabagh. A caravan of about eighty warriors who were under the leadership of ‘Ubaidah bin Harith # were also there.

Miqdad bin Aswad and ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan were members of the caravan which had come from Makkah. The leader of the caravan, ‘Ikrimah was under the impression that because of family pride they

had accompanied him and would certainly be of some help. But when fighting broke out between the two forces of Good and Evil and the army of ‘Ikrimah was defeated; the two intelligent and pure-hearted youths took this opportunity to join up with the Mujahideen. Thus they reached Al-Madinah and gained the privilege of presenting themselves before the Prophet 3% and also got the distinction of migrating twice for the cause of Islam.

‘Utbah bin Ghazwan # played a very active role in all the Ghazawét.' His courage and daring were well known and his exploits were common topics of conversation. He showed great acumen, wise insight and expertise in planning strategies of war.

One night ‘Umar Farooq 4 lay down to rest after his ‘Isha prayers, so that he could go out on his nightly incognito tours of the city to see that all was well with the Ummah. But he was not able to take a nap because the critical conditions in the war being fought on the Iranian borders was troubling him. The messenger of war had brought the news that whenever the Muslim army gathered strength to launch a powerful attack and defeat him, the enemy would get reinforcements and renew itself to face them. Caliph ‘Umar Farooq was also told that reinforcements were reaching the Iranian forces from the city of Ablah. He made a firm decision to send an army to conquer Ablah and thus close off the route through which reinforcements were reaching the Iranian army.

But he had a scarcity of manpower, since the youths and the old men had already left their homes and gone for Jihdd. Thus very few men were left in Al-Madinah. On this occasion he used his celebrated secret weapon! He decided that using experienced commanders should make up for the shortage of soldiers. He took up the list of all the individuals present in Al-Madinah and examined it from the point of view of their military expertise and experience.

After a long period of reflection and consideration, he jumped up

' In those battles the Prophet 3 took part.

saying: “| have found him, yes, I have found him!”

Then again lying down he said to himself, ‘This is a Mujahid who has played a very prominent role in the battles of Badr, Uhud, Khandaq and in many others. His arrows and sword have never missed their aim. And he has also had the honour of migrating twice - to Ethiopia and to Al-Madinah. And he was one among those first fortunate Muslims who accepted Islam.

He then sent an army of three hundred and nineteen Mujahideen under his Command of ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan to Ablah. He promised him that he would soon send a large number of Mujahideen for his support. When the army got ready to leave he told them something of very great importance.

“Utbah I am sending you towards Ablah. This is a very important and powerful stronghold of the enemy. My hope is with Allah that He will help you. When you reach your destination, first invite the citizens to accept Islam. If they accept, they are your brothers. If they refuse to accept Islam, then demand Jizyah. If they refuse to pay Jizyah declare war on them. ‘Utbah fear Allah in fulfilling the responsibilities of your office. Avoid arrogance, haughtiness and boastfulness. Such habits are responsible for ruining your life in the Hereafter. You must bear in mind that it is due to the great goodness of Allah 3% that you have received the benefits of moving with the Prophet 38. Allah 3& has granted you greatness and strength after frailty. At this time you are a popular General; all that you say will be listened to with respect, all your orders will be obeyed happily and willingly. Do not make the mistake of misusing this bounty of Allah 3%, else you will regret it on the Day of Judgement. May Allah keep you and me in His Protection. Ameen.”

“As were walking along searching for something eatable, we entered a small grove. There we saw two earthen pots. One was filled with dates and the other one had small yellow grains in it. We picked up both the pots and carried them to our Commander. One of our companions glanced at it and said they should be very careful, for it might be poison which the enemy had left behind to get rid of them. We all turned towards the dates and started eating them. One of our horses, meanwhile, broke his tether and went straight to the pot with the grains, put his mouth in and started to eat them with great enjoyment. By God, we thought we should kill the horse as it might suffer with the poison and die eventually. But one of our friends said we should wait and see. Anyway he was going to keep watch at night, if the horse seemed to be in pain he would kill him. When morning came they saw that nothing had happened to the horse and it was fine!
content_id:
Aku berdiri tegak dan tubuhku gemetar. Kemudian beliau memerintahkanku:

"Segera kembali ke Ahwaz dan begitu kau tiba di sana, bagikan mutiara-mutiara ini kepada para mujahidin."

Dengan penuh hormat aku berkata, 'Khalifah, aku akan mematuhi perintahmu.' Beliau memandangku dengan penuh murka dan berkata:

"Demi Allah, jika kalung ini tidak dibagikan kepada para mujahidin, aku akan menghancurkan tulang-tulangmu dan juga tulang-tulang Panglima Besar itu."

Aku memacu kudaku dengan kecepatan penuh dan melewati jalan-jalan yang sulit itu, hingga akhirnya tiba di hadapan Panglima Besar Salamah bin Qais. Aku menceritakan apa yang telah terjadi dan berkata:

"Wahai Salamah, jika engkau ingin menyelamatkan nyawamu dan nyawaku, maka segera bagikan kalung ini kepada para mujahidin."

Beliau bertanya kepadaku apa yang telah terjadi dan mengapa aku begitu ketakutan. Lalu aku menceritakan semua rincian tentang apa yang telah terjadi padaku.

Salamah bin Qais membagikan kalung itu di majlis (perkumpulan) para mujahidin saat itu juga.

'Utbah bin Ghazwan

"Aku memohon perlindungan Allah dari keadaan

di mana aku mungkin menganggap diriku sangat hebat sekarang, namun di sisi Allah aku akan menjadi

'Utbah bin Ghazwan

Tinggi dan bertubuh tegap, dengan wajah bercahaya, kepribadian yang menarik, dan postur yang kokoh - itulah penampilan fisiknya. Beliau memiliki adab yang halus dan merupakan seorang yang menjalankan zuhud, menjalani kehidupan yang bersih dan saleh. Di medan perang, beliau adalah prajurit pemberani yang mampu memberikan pukulan mematikan demi perjuangan Islam.

Pada tahun 14 Hijriah, dalam ketaatan terhadap perintah 'Umar Farooq, beliau membangun sebuah kota baru yang indah, Basrah, di tepi sungai Dajlah. Beliau adalah pemimpin yang membimbing kaumnya dengan bijaksana dan baik. Beliau menunjukkan keahliannya dengan pedang sebagai mujahid dalam Perang Khandaq, serta Perang Badr, Uhud, Khaibar, dan Tha'if. Beliau juga termasuk di antara mereka yang turut serta dalam penandatanganan Perjanjian Hudaibiah dan merupakan salah seorang yang beruntung dan terhormat yang ikut dalam Bai'at Ridhwan.

Beliau adalah tokoh besar ummah Muslim yang memasuki Makkah dengan penuh kemenangan namun rendah hati bersama Nabi ﷺ dan sepuluh ribu mujahidin setia lainnya yang telah mempertaruhkan nyawa mereka. Dalam Perang Tabuk, beliau adalah prajurit yang tekun dan merupakan salah satu anggota 'Jaisy Al-'Usrah'. Beliau meninggalkan kesan mendalam akan keberanian dan kepahlawanannya sebagai seorang Jenderal. 'Umar Farooq memilihnya untuk memimpin pasukan menaklukkan wilayah selatan Irak setelah pertimbangan yang matang. Beliau memerintahkannya untuk memimpin kontingen kecil pasukan Muslim ke Irak dan Iran. Inilah nasihat beliau pada kesempatan itu:

"Wahai 'Utbah, capailah batas-batas wilayah Arab yang berbatasan dengan tanah non-Arab, pimpin pasukanmu dengan tawakkul penuh kepada Allah. Selalu ingat untuk menjaga rasa takut kepada Allah di hadapanmu. Kau akan menghadapi musuh yang licik, penuh tipu daya, dan

munafik. Kau harus menjaga pikiran yang waspada; berani dan bijaksana dalam menghadapinya. Pergilah dan semoga Allah menjadi Penolong dan Penolongmu."

'Utbah bin Ghazwan adalah seorang pemuda yang cerdas dan populer dari suku Banu Mazan. Beliau adalah kebanggaan dan kekaguman kaumnya. Pada masa Jahiliah, keluarganya merupakan sekutu dari suku Banu Nofil bin 'Abd Manaf. Ketika beliau berusia tiga puluh tahun, berita beredar di Makkah bahwa putra kesayangan Aminah, cucu 'Abdul-Muththalib dan putra 'Abdullah telah menyatakan bahwa beliau adalah seorang Nabi. Risalahnya adalah bahwa hanya ada Satu Allah yang Maha Benar. Hanya Dialah Pencipta dan Penguasa, dan hanya Dialah Pemberi Rezeki dan Penolong, dan hanya Dialah yang berkuasa atas hidup dan mati. Ketika klaim beliau menjadi diketahui, terjadi kegemparan yang dahsyat di Makkah.

Para pemuka Quraisy mulai melakukan kekejaman yang paling kejam terhadap kaum Muslim yang baru masuk Islam. Melihat ketidakberdayaan para pengikutnya, Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan mereka untuk berhijrah ke Ethiopia. 'Utbah bin Ghazwan termasuk anggota rombongan kedua dari kaum yang tertindas yang meninggalkan Makkah. Setelah tinggal di sana dalam kedamaian dan keamanan selama beberapa waktu, beliau kembali, karena merindukan Nabi ﷺ yang sangat dicintainya dan juga karena merasa gelisah dan rindu kampung halaman.

Ketika Nabi ﷺ memutuskan untuk berhijrah ke Al-Madinah karena kekejaman Quraisy sudah tidak tertahankan lagi, 'Utbah bin Ghazwan kembali merindukannya. Beliau menunggu kesempatan untuk menikmati persahabatan dan kebersamaan dengan Nabi tercintanya ﷺ sesegera mungkin.

Pada bulan Syawwal, 1 Hijriah, beberapa pemuda bersenjata berangkat di bawah pimpinan 'Ikrimah bin Abu Jahl untuk menekan kaum Muslim yang baru menetap. Setelah melakukan perjalanan beberapa waktu, mereka tiba di suatu tempat bernama Rabagh. Sebuah rombongan yang terdiri dari sekitar delapan puluh pejuang di bawah pimpinan 'Ubaidah bin Harits juga berada di sana.

Miqdad bin Aswad dan 'Utbah bin Ghazwan adalah anggota rombongan yang datang dari Makkah. Pemimpin rombongan, 'Ikrimah, mengira bahwa karena kebanggaan keluarga, mereka

telah menemaninya dan pasti akan memberikan bantuan. Namun ketika pertempuran pecah antara dua kekuatan Kebaikan dan Kejahatan dan pasukan 'Ikrimah dikalahkan, kedua pemuda yang cerdas dan berhati suci ini mengambil kesempatan untuk bergabung dengan para mujahidin. Dengan demikian mereka tiba di Al-Madinah dan mendapatkan kehormatan untuk menghadap Nabi ﷺ dan juga mendapatkan kemuliaan berhijrah dua kali demi perjuangan Islam.

'Utbah bin Ghazwan memainkan peran yang sangat aktif dalam semua ghazawat.' Keberanian dan kepahlawanannya sangat terkenal dan kisah-kisah kepahlawanannya menjadi bahan pembicaraan umum. Beliau menunjukkan ketajaman, wawasan yang bijak, dan keahlian dalam merencanakan strategi perang.

Suatu malam 'Umar Farooq berbaring untuk beristirahat setelah shalat 'Isya, agar beliau bisa keluar untuk tur malam menyamar mengelilingi kota untuk memastikan semua berjalan baik bagi ummah. Namun beliau tidak bisa tidur sejenak pun karena kondisi kritis dalam perang yang sedang berkecamuk di perbatasan Iran mengganggu pikirannya. Utusan perang membawa berita bahwa setiap kali pasukan Muslim mengumpulkan kekuatan untuk melancarkan serangan dahsyat dan mengalahkannya, musuh mendapatkan bala bantuan dan memperbarui kekuatannya untuk menghadapi mereka. Khalifah 'Umar Farooq juga diberitahu bahwa bala bantuan terus mengalir ke pasukan Iran dari kota Ablah. Beliau membuat keputusan tegas untuk mengirim pasukan menaklukkan Ablah dan dengan demikian memutus jalur yang dilalui bala bantuan untuk mencapai pasukan Iran.

Namun beliau kekurangan tenaga, karena para pemuda dan orang-orang tua sudah meninggalkan rumah mereka dan pergi berjihad. Sehingga sangat sedikit orang yang tersisa di Al-Madinah. Pada kesempatan ini beliau menggunakan senjata rahasianya yang terkenal! Beliau memutuskan bahwa menggunakan para panglima berpengalaman seharusnya bisa menutupi kekurangan jumlah prajurit. Beliau mengambil daftar semua individu yang ada di Al-Madinah dan memeriksanya dari sudut pandang keahlian dan pengalaman militer mereka.

Setelah waktu yang lama merenung dan mempertimbangkan, beliau melompat bangun

' Peperangan-peperangan yang Nabi ﷺ turut serta di dalamnya.

seraya berkata: "Aku telah menemukannya, ya, aku telah menemukannya!"

Kemudian berbaring kembali, beliau berkata pada dirinya sendiri, 'Ini adalah seorang mujahid yang telah memainkan peran yang sangat menonjol dalam Perang Badr, Uhud, Khandaq, dan banyak peperangan lainnya. Anak panah dan pedangnya tidak pernah meleset dari sasarannya. Dan beliau juga mendapatkan kehormatan berhijrah dua kali - ke Ethiopia dan ke Al-Madinah. Dan beliau termasuk di antara orang-orang Muslim pertama yang beruntung menerima Islam.

Kemudian beliau mengirim pasukan yang terdiri dari tiga ratus sembilan belas mujahidin di bawah Komando 'Utbah bin Ghazwan ke Ablah. Beliau menjanjikannya bahwa akan segera mengirim sejumlah besar mujahidin untuk mendukungnya. Ketika pasukan siap berangkat, beliau menyampaikan sesuatu yang sangat penting kepada mereka.

"'Utbah, aku mengirimmu menuju Ablah. Ini adalah benteng musuh yang sangat penting dan kuat. Harapanku ada pada Allah bahwa Dia akan menolongmu. Ketika kau tiba di tujuanmu, pertama-tama ajaklah penduduk untuk menerima Islam. Jika mereka menerima, mereka adalah saudara-saudaramu. Jika mereka menolak menerima Islam, maka tuntutlah jizyah. Jika mereka menolak membayar jizyah, nyatakan perang terhadap mereka. 'Utbah, takutlah kepada Allah dalam memenuhi tanggung jawab jabatanmu. Hindarilah kesombongan, keangkuhan, dan sikap membanggakan diri. Kebiasaan-kebiasaan semacam itu bertanggung jawab atas rusaknya kehidupanmu di akhirat. Kau harus mengingat bahwa karena kebaikan Allah yang besar engkau telah menerima manfaat bergerak bersama Nabi ﷺ. Allah telah menganugerahimu kebesaran dan kekuatan setelah kelemahan. Saat ini kau adalah Jenderal yang populer; semua yang kau katakan akan didengarkan dengan penuh hormat, semua perintahmu akan dipatuhi dengan senang hati dan sukarela. Jangan membuat kesalahan dengan menyalahgunakan nikmat Allah ini, jika tidak kau akan menyesalinya pada Hari Kiamat. Semoga Allah menjaga engkau dan aku dalam Perlindungan-Nya. Amin."

"Saat kami berjalan mencari sesuatu yang bisa dimakan, kami memasuki sebuah kebun kecil. Di sana kami melihat dua pot tanah liat. Satu berisi kurma dan yang lainnya berisi biji-bijian kecil berwarna kuning. Kami mengambil kedua pot itu dan membawanya kepada Komandan kami. Salah seorang sahabat kami melihatnya dan berkata bahwa mereka harus sangat berhati-hati, karena bisa jadi itu adalah racun yang ditinggalkan musuh untuk membinasakan mereka. Kami semua beralih ke kurma dan mulai memakannya. Sementara itu, salah satu kuda kami melepaskan talinya dan langsung menuju pot berisi biji-bijian itu, memasukkan mulutnya dan mulai memakannya dengan sangat lahap. Demi Allah, kami berpikir untuk membunuh kuda itu karena mungkin ia akan menderita keracunan dan akhirnya mati. Tetapi salah seorang teman kami berkata agar kami menunggu dan melihat dulu. Bagaimanapun, ia akan berjaga di malam hari, jika kuda itu tampak kesakitan ia akan membunuhnya. Ketika pagi tiba, mereka melihat bahwa tidak terjadi apa-apa pada kuda itu dan ia baik-baik saja!

His Message is that there is only One True الله. Only He is the Creator and the Master, and only He is the Provider and Helper and only He has control over الحياة and الموت. عندما His claims became known there was a terrible tumult in مكة المكرمة. Seeing the helplessness of his followers, النبي ﷺ 3 ordered them to migrate towards Ethiopia. “Utbah bin Ghazwan 4 was a member of the ثانياً caravan of the oppressed people which left مكة المكرمة. بعد living there in peace and security for some time he returned, as he was missing النبي ﷺ 38 whom he loved deeply and أيضاً he was feeling restless and homesick. عندما النبي ﷺ 3% decided to migrate to Al-المدينة المنورة لأن the cruelty of the Quraish had become intolerable, ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan again started to miss him. The leader of the caravan, ‘Ikrimah was under the impression that لأن of الأسرة الكبر they had accompanied him and would certainly be of some help. But عندما fighting broke out between the two forces of الخير and الشر and the الجيش of ‘Ikrimah was الهزيمةed; the two intelligent and pure-hearted youths took this opportunity to join up with the المجاهدين. وهكذا they reached Al-المدينة المنورة and gained the privilege of presenting themselves قبل النبي ﷺ 3% and أيضاً got the distinction of migrating twice for the cause of الإسلام. ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan # played a very active role in all the Ghazawét.' His الشجاعة and daring were well known and his exploits were common topics of conversation. He showed great acumen, wise insight and expertise in planning strategies of war. One night ‘عمر Farooq 4 lay down to rest بعد his ‘Isha الصلوات, لذا that he could go out on his nightly incognito tours of the city to see that all was well with the الأمة. But he was not able to take a nap لأن the البالغ الأهمية conditions in the war being قاتل on the Iranian borders was troubling him. الرسول ﷺ of war had brought the news that كلما the مسلم الجيش gathered القوة to launch a powerful attack and الهزيمة him, the العدو would get reinforcements and renew itself to face them. Caliph ‘عمر Farooq was أيضاً told that reinforcements were reaching the Iranian forces from the city of Ablah. He made a firm decision to send an الجيش to conquer Ablah and وهكذا close off the route through which reinforcements were reaching the Iranian الجيش. But he had a scarcity of manpower, بما أن the youths and the old men had already left their homes and gone for Jihdd. وهكذا very few men were left in Al-المدينة المنورة. On this occasion he used his celebrated secret weapon! He decided that using experienced القائدs should make up for the shortage of soldiers. He took up the list of all the individuals present in Al-المدينة المنورة and examined it from the point of view of their military expertise and experience. بعد a long period of reflection and consideration, he jumped up ' In those المعركةs النبي ﷺ 3 took part. saying: “| have found him, yes, I have found him!” Then again lying down قال to himself, ‘This is a Mujahid who has played a very prominent role in the المعركةs of Badr, Uhud, Khandaq and in many others. His arrows and السيف have never missed their aim. And he has أيضاً had the honour of migrating twice - to Ethiopia and to Al-المدينة المنورة. And he was one among those أولاً fortunate المسلمون who accepted الإسلام. He then sent an الجيش of three hundred and nineteen المجاهدين under his Command of ‘Utbah bin Ghazwan to Ablah. He promised him that he would soon send a large number of المجاهدين for his support. عندما the الجيش got ready to leave he told them something of very great importance. “Utbah I am sending you towards Ablah. This is a very المهم and powerful stronghold of the العدو. My الأمل is with الله that He will help you. عندما you reach your destination, أولاً invite the citizens to accept الإسلام. إذا they accept, they are your brothers. إذا they refuse to accept الإسلام, then demand Jizyah. إذا they refuse to pay Jizyah declare war on them. ‘Utbah الخوف الله in fulfilling the responsibilities of your office. Avoid الغرور, haughtiness and boastfulness. Such habits are responsible for ruining your الحياة in الآخرة. You must bear in العقل that it is بسبب the great goodness of الله 3% that you have received the benefits of moving with النبي ﷺ 38. الله 3& has granted you greatness and القوة بعد frailty. At this time you are a popular General; all that you say will be listened to with الاحترام, all your orders will be obeyed happily and willingly. Do not make the mistake of misusing this bounty of الله 3%, else you will regret it on the يوم القيامة. May الله keep you and me in His Protection. آمين.” “As were walking along searching for something eatable, we entered a small grove. There we saw two earthen pots. One was filled with dates and the other one had small yellow grains in it. We picked up both the pots and carried them to our القائد. One of our الصحابة glanced at it and said they should be very careful, for it might be poison which the العدو had left behind to get rid of them. We all turned towards the dates and started eating them. One of our horses, meanwhile, broke his tether and went straight to the pot with the grains, put his mouth in and started to eat them with great enjoyment. By God, we thought ينبغي لنا kill the horse as it might suffer with the poison and die eventually. But one of our friends said ينبغي لنا wait and see. Anyway he was going to keep watch at night, إذا the horse seemed to be in الألم he would kill him. عندما morning came they saw that nothing had happened to the horse and it was fine!
